wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Skylink Custom Backend project overview. Explain the system's purpose as an integrated HR and attendance management platform, its key features including user management, attendance tracking, leave management, payroll processing, and payment operations. Document the technology stack (Spring Boot 3.4.0, Java 21, PostgreSQL), architecture overview, and target audience. Include system benefits, use cases, and high-level component relationships. Provide both conceptual understanding for stakeholders and technical foundation for developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication system component. Document the Spring Security configuration including form-based authentication, custom authentication success handlers, login/logout processes, and remember-me functionality. Explain the BCrypt password encoding implementation, session management, and security filter chain configuration. Include practical examples of authentication flows, security configurations, and integration with the login controller. Address security best practices, credential validation, and authentication failure handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for employee CRUD operations in the Skylink Custom Backend. Document the complete employee management workflow including create, read, update, and delete operations. Explain the EmployeeController implementation with REST endpoints and Thymeleaf templates. Cover employee data validation, password encoding, and form handling. Include practical examples of employee creation forms, search functionality, pagination, sorting, and bulk operations. Document the integration with department selection, supervisor assignment, and resource management. Address error handling, data consistency, and security considerations for employee data manipulation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Request Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for leave request processing workflows. Document the complete lifecycle from employee application submission to approval/rejection. Explain request validation rules, leave type restrictions, date range validation, and overlap detection with existing leave requests. Cover the service layer implementation including business logic for leave calculations, entitlement verification, and policy enforcement. Include practical examples of request creation, modification, cancellation, and deletion processes. Address integration with employee profiles, attendance tracking, and payroll systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system overview documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Explain the overall system architecture, technology stack (Spring Boot 3.4.0, Java 21, PostgreSQL), and high-level design principles. Document the monolithic architecture approach, component relationships, and system boundaries. Include system requirements, deployment topology, and integration points with external systems like ADMS devices and email providers. Provide architectural decision rationale and scalability considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Request Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for payment request management functionality. Document the complete payment request lifecycle including creation, submission, review, approval, and tracking. Explain the multi-tier approval workflow with role-based access control for Admin, HR, and Supervisors. Detail the request form validation, master data integration with contractors, clients, and payment methods. Cover the filtering and sorting capabilities, search functionality, and bulk operations. Include practical examples of payment request creation, status updates, rejection workflows, and audit trails. Address security restrictions, update limits, and notification mechanisms for request status changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Salary Calculation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the salary calculation engine component. Document the core algorithms used for salary computation including basic salary calculation, attendance-based adjustments, leave impact calculations, and overtime processing. Explain the integration with attendance logs, leave requests, and employee contracts. Include mathematical formulas, calculation precedence, rounding rules, and edge case handling. Document configurable parameters such as daily wage calculation, penalty calculations for late arrivals, bonus computation, and deduction algorithms. Provide examples of different salary structures and calculation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for WebSocket real-time communication implementation. Document WebSocket configuration, message handling patterns, bidirectional communication setup, and live update mechanisms. Explain connection establishment, message broadcasting, session management, and integration with notification system. Include practical examples of WebSocket endpoints, message formats, client-side integration patterns, and real-time status updates for attendance monitoring and work order tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Attendance Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for real-time attendance tracking functionality. Document the attendance logging mechanism, timestamp management, employee status updates, and live monitoring capabilities. Explain the AttendanceLog entity structure, repository operations, and DTO transformations for frontend consumption. Include practical examples of attendance data retrieval, filtering by date ranges, employee status checking, and integration with WebSocket real-time updates. Address performance considerations for high-volume attendance data and caching strategies for frequently accessed attendance records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Import & Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the data import and export functionality in Skylink Custom Backend. Document the CSV import/export endpoints, supported data types (employees, departments, leaves, devices, settings, users, workorders), file upload handling, and data transformation processes. Explain the DataManagementController implementation, multipart file processing, error handling for empty files and unknown types, and redirect responses. Include practical examples of importing/exporting different data entities, CSV format specifications, validation rules applied during import, and integration patterns with external systems. Address performance considerations for large datasets, batch processing capabilities, and data integrity validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity model documentation for all JPA entities in the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail each entity's purpose, fields, data types, annotations, and business significance. Document entity lifecycle, validation rules, and business logic embedded in models. Include field descriptions for Employee, Department, AttendanceLog, Payslip, LeaveRequest, WorkOrder, Client, Contractor, PaymentRequest, Notification, and User entities. Explain Lombok annotations usage, JPA annotations, and entity constraints. Provide examples of entity relationships and data mapping patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for authentication endpoints including login, logout, password reset, and user registration. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication mechanisms. Include examples of login requests with credentials, session management, token handling, and role-based access patterns. Address security considerations, CSRF protection, and session timeout handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive environment configuration documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail the application.properties file structure, environment-specific property files (application-dev.properties and application-prod.properties), and configuration management patterns. Document database connection settings, server configuration, logging levels, and environment-specific overrides. Include practical examples of switching between development and production environments, property precedence rules, and common configuration scenarios. Address configuration validation, property naming conventions, and troubleshooting environment-specific issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Include prerequisites (JDK 21, PostgreSQL 15+, Gradle), step-by-step installation instructions, database configuration setup, environment variable configuration, and initial application startup. Document both development and production environment setup, explain the build process using Gradle wrapper, and provide troubleshooting tips for common setup issues. Include verification steps to ensure successful installation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authorization and Roles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authorization and role-based access control system. Detail the five user roles (ADMIN, HR, SUPERVISOR, EMPLOYEE, COMPANY) and their specific permissions and access levels. Document the role hierarchy, permission matrix, and URL-based access control patterns. Explain role-based method security, custom access decision voters, and dynamic permission checking. Include practical examples of role assignments, permission enforcement, and access control scenarios across different system modules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Department Hierarchy Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for department hierarchy management in the Skylink Custom Backend. Document department CRUD operations, organizational structure maintenance, and hierarchical relationships. Explain department creation, modification, and deletion processes. Cover department-to-employee associations, reporting structures, and organizational chart management. Include practical examples of department navigation, filtering, and visualization. Document department-specific permissions, resource allocation, and integration with employee management workflows. Address department data validation, inheritance patterns, and organizational policy enforcement.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for leave approval workflows and hierarchical approval systems. Document role-based approval routing including admin, HR, and supervisor approval chains. Explain approval permissions, delegation mechanisms, and team-based approval hierarchies. Cover the approval decision logic, comment requirements, and audit trail generation. Include examples of different approval scenarios, escalation procedures, and approval status transitions. Address security checks, access control enforcement, and approval workflow customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layered Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the layered architecture pattern implementation. Document the clear separation between presentation layer (controllers), business logic layer (services), and data access layer (repositories). Explain how each layer interacts with adjacent layers, data flow patterns, and responsibility distribution. Include examples of layer boundaries, dependency injection usage, and transaction management across layers. Address how this architecture supports maintainability, testability, and scalability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the payment dashboard functionality. Document the dashboard statistics, KPI tracking, and real-time payment metrics display. Explain the aggregated data visualization, payment status distribution charts, and performance indicators. Cover the dashboard customization options, filter capabilities, and export features. Include examples of dashboard configuration, metric calculations, and data refresh mechanisms. Address the integration with payment analytics, trend analysis, and reporting dashboards for different user roles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payslip Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the payslip generation system. Detail the payslip creation workflow from raw payroll data to finalized payslips. Document payslip structure, field definitions, formatting rules, and PDF generation capabilities. Explain payslip lifecycle stages including draft creation, review processes, approval workflows, and finalization. Include payslip templates, customization options, and branding features. Document batch processing capabilities, error handling during generation, and retry mechanisms. Address payslip validation rules, audit trails, and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Push Notification Service
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive push notification service documentation using Web Push Protocol. Detail VAPID (Voluntary Application-Server Identification) configuration, push subscription management, browser registration handling, and notification delivery mechanisms. Document subscription creation, endpoint management, payload encryption, and cross-browser compatibility. Include practical examples of push subscription workflows, notification payload formats, error handling strategies, and integration with attendance status updates and work order changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: ADMS Device Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for ADMS device integration system. Detail the communication protocols, command queuing mechanism, device status monitoring, and data synchronization processes. Document the Device entity model, ADMS command structure (DATA QUERY ATTLOG, DATA QUERY USERINFO), and batch processing workflows. Include practical examples of device configuration, command execution, error handling, and troubleshooting common ADMS connectivity issues. Explain the integration architecture between the backend and physical attendance devices, including network communication patterns and data validation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the report generation system in Skylink Custom Backend. Document the ReportService implementation, attendance reporting formats (daily, weekly, monthly), DTO structures for report data transfer, and report generation workflows. Explain the different report types including employee attendance summaries, department performance metrics, and payroll-related reports. Include practical examples of generating attendance reports, configuring report parameters, filtering criteria, and export formats. Address report data aggregation algorithms, date range handling, timezone considerations, and performance optimization for large report datasets. Document report caching strategies, scheduled report generation, and integration with the export system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Relationships and Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for database relationships and constraints in the Skylink Custom Backend. Document foreign key relationships between entities, cascade operations, and referential integrity rules. Explain many-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships with practical examples. Detail constraint definitions, unique constraints, and business rule enforcement through database schema. Include relationship mapping patterns, join table configurations, and query optimization strategies for complex relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for employee CRUD operations, department management, and supervisor relationships. Document endpoints for employee creation, updates, deletion, and retrieval. Include department hierarchy operations, supervisor assignment, and employee search/filtering. Provide request/response schemas for employee profiles, department structures, and relationship management. Include examples of bulk operations and data validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security configuration documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Document the SecurityConfig class implementation, role-based access control (RBAC) patterns, authentication mechanisms, and authorization rules. Detail the custom authentication success handler, form-based login configuration, remember-me functionality, and logout procedures. Include comprehensive examples of URL pattern matching, role hierarchies, and security filter chain configuration. Address security best practices, CSRF configuration decisions, and troubleshooting authentication issues. Document integration with Spring Security components and custom security implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture and Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend system. Describe the layered architecture (presentation, business, data), Spring Boot MVC pattern implementation, component interactions, and integration patterns. Document the security architecture with Spring Security, WebSocket real-time communication setup, Thymeleaf templating integration, and REST API design principles. Include system boundaries, cross-cutting concerns like authentication, authorization, and notification systems. Explain technical decisions, architectural patterns used, and scalability considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user profile management functionality. Document user CRUD operations, profile update processes, user registration workflows, and user data validation. Explain the User entity structure, field mappings, and relationships with other entities. Include practical examples of user management operations, profile customization options, and integration with the user details service. Address user data security, profile synchronization, and administrative user management capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Organizational Structure & Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for organizational structure and reporting relationships in the Skylink Custom Backend. Document supervisor-employee relationships, reporting hierarchies, and chain of command management. Explain the reports-to and reports-to-assistant relationships in the Employee model. Cover organizational chart visualization, team management workflows, and delegation patterns. Include practical examples of team structure maintenance, supervisor permissions, and reporting workflows. Document integration with leave approvals, attendance monitoring, and performance tracking. Address organizational policy enforcement, approval workflows, and administrative controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Calendar Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for leave calendar management and visualization systems. Document the integration with FullCalendar for displaying approved leave requests across the organization. Explain calendar event generation, color-coding by leave types, and date range display logic. Cover calendar filtering options, search functionality, and export capabilities. Include examples of calendar integration with employee dashboards, team views, and administrative oversight. Address calendar synchronization with external systems and real-time updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: MVC Pattern Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Model-View-Controller pattern implementation in the Spring Boot application. Document how controllers handle HTTP requests, service layer manages business logic, and views (Thymeleaf templates) render server-side HTML. Explain the role of WebConfig in static resource serving and WebSocketConfig for real-time communication. Include request-response flow examples, controller responsibilities, and view resolution mechanisms. Address how this pattern supports separation of concerns and maintainable web application development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Master Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for master data management in payment operations. Document the maintenance of clients, contractors, and payment methods used in payment requests. Explain the data validation rules, active/inactive status management, and hierarchical relationships. Cover CRUD operations for master data entities, bulk import/export capabilities, and data integrity constraints. Include examples of master data setup, relationship mapping, and integration with payment request forms. Address data quality controls, duplicate detection, and audit trails for master data changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advance Salary Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for advance salary processing functionality. Document the complete workflow for employee advance requests including application submission, approval processes, and repayment mechanisms. Explain advance calculation methods, repayment schedules, and deduction integration with regular payroll. Detail approval hierarchies, authorization limits, and workflow automation. Include advance request forms, approval routing, notification systems, and audit trails. Document integration with payroll deduction systems and impact on monthly salary calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for email integration service implementation. Document SMTP configuration, email template management, notification dispatching, and email queue handling. Explain email content formatting, attachment handling, bulk email processing, and delivery status tracking. Include practical examples of email notification workflows, template customization, error handling for failed deliveries, and integration with payroll notifications, leave approvals, and system alerts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance Historical Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for attendance historical data management and reporting. Document attendance log archiving, data aggregation functions, monthly summaries, and detailed employee attendance reports. Explain the repository query patterns, pagination strategies, and data export capabilities. Include practical examples of generating attendance reports, filtering historical data by date ranges and employee categories, and integrating attendance data with payroll calculations. Address data retention policies, performance optimization for large datasets, and efficient querying patterns for attendance analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bulk Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for bulk data operations in Skylink Custom Backend. Document bulk import/export capabilities, batch processing workflows, and data manipulation operations. Explain the DataImportExportService implementation for handling large-scale data operations, transaction management for bulk updates, and error handling strategies for partial failures. Include practical examples of bulk employee imports, department updates, leave request processing, and device management operations. Address performance optimization techniques for bulk operations, memory management considerations, progress tracking, and rollback mechanisms. Document bulk operation validation rules, data consistency checks, and integration with the main application data flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Indexing and Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive indexing and performance optimization documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend database layer. Detail indexing strategies for frequently queried columns, composite indexes for complex queries, and performance tuning techniques. Document query optimization patterns, repository method naming conventions, and JPQL/HQL optimization. Include database query performance monitoring, execution plan analysis, and bottleneck identification. Address pagination strategies, batch processing, and memory optimization for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for attendance tracking endpoints including real-time attendance logging, device integration, and attendance history. Document endpoints for checking in/check out, attendance status updates, device communication protocols, and server time synchronization. Include request/response formats for attendance records, device status, and batch operations. Address ADMS device integration, timestamp handling, and attendance validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive WebSocket configuration documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail the WebSocketConfig class implementation, message broker configuration, STOMP endpoint registration, and real-time communication setup. Document WebSocket message handling patterns, topic subscriptions, user-specific messaging, and broadcast mechanisms. Include practical examples of WebSocket endpoint configuration, message conversion, and integration with Spring's messaging infrastructure. Address WebSocket security considerations, connection handling, and troubleshooting real-time communication issues. Document the relationship between WebSocket configuration and notification services.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive user management documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail the authentication and authorization system including role-based access control (ADMIN, EMPLOYEE, SUPERVISOR, COMPANY), custom authentication success handlers, password encryption with BCrypt, and session management. Document user registration processes, profile management, role assignment, and security configurations. Include practical examples of user workflows, security best practices, and integration with other system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Configurations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for security configuration and security-related settings. Detail the Spring Security filter chain configuration, CSRF protection settings, CORS configuration, and security headers. Document the global exception handling for security-related errors, custom security configurations, and integration with external security services. Include practical examples of security configuration best practices, threat mitigation strategies, and security audit logging. Address security monitoring, intrusion detection, and security incident response procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Import & Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for employee import and export functionality in the Skylink Custom Backend. Document CSV and Excel file processing for bulk employee data operations. Explain import validation rules, data transformation, and error handling mechanisms. Cover export formats, report generation, and data formatting options. Include practical examples of employee data migration, bulk updates, and data synchronization. Document supported file formats, column mappings, and validation constraints. Address data integrity checks, duplicate detection, and conflict resolution during imports. Explain export templates, filtering options, and batch processing capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Policy Enforcement
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for leave policy enforcement mechanisms and business rule implementation. Document leave type definitions, entitlement calculations, carry-forward policies, and usage limitations. Explain policy validation during request processing, quota checking, and conflict resolution. Cover policy configuration options, rule customization, and compliance monitoring. Include examples of policy scenarios, validation rules, and enforcement mechanisms. Address integration with employee contracts, company policies, and regulatory requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security architecture documentation covering Spring Security implementation, authentication mechanisms, and authorization patterns. Document multi-role authentication (Admin, HR, Supervisor, Employee, Company), custom authentication success handlers, and user details service implementation. Explain password encryption with BCrypt, session management, CSRF protection, and CORS configuration. Include security filter chain configuration, custom access control rules, and integration with external authentication systems. Address security best practices and vulnerability mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting and Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for payment reporting and export capabilities. Document the export formats including CSV and PDF generation, column selection options, and data filtering for exports. Explain the payment history reporting, invoice generation, and batch processing features. Cover the report customization options, scheduling capabilities, and automated export workflows. Include examples of report generation, export configuration, and integration with external accounting systems. Address performance considerations for large dataset exports and data transformation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payroll Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive payroll reporting documentation covering all reporting capabilities within the system. Detail monthly payroll summaries, department-wise reports, individual employee statements, and financial insights dashboards. Document report generation workflows, filtering options, export capabilities, and scheduled reporting. Explain key metrics calculation including YTD earnings, bonus tracking, income trends, and payroll analytics. Include report customization, sharing options, and integration with external accounting systems. Address report security, access controls, and compliance reporting requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive notification management documentation covering notification lifecycle, storage mechanisms, and retrieval patterns. Detail notification types, priority handling, read/unread status management, and notification history tracking. Document notification creation workflows, user-specific filtering, pagination strategies, and bulk operations. Include practical examples of notification routing, user preference handling, notification aggregation, and integration with WebSocket and push notification services for multi-channel delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Device Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for device management functionality. Detail device registration, configuration, status monitoring, and administrative operations. Document the Device entity structure, CRUD operations, device status tracking, and integration with attendance systems. Include practical examples of adding new devices, updating device configurations, removing obsolete devices, and monitoring device health. Explain device synchronization processes, error handling for disconnected devices, and best practices for device maintenance and troubleshooting common hardware issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Validation & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for data validation and quality assurance mechanisms in Skylink Custom Backend. Document validation rules applied during data import, data transformation processes, error detection and handling, and data quality checks. Explain the validation logic for different data types (employee records, attendance logs, leave requests, etc.), constraint checking, duplicate detection, and referential integrity validation. Include practical examples of validation scenarios, error reporting mechanisms, data correction workflows, and audit trail generation. Address validation performance optimization, batch validation strategies, and integration with the import/export processes. Document data profiling capabilities, quality metrics, and automated validation reporting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Evolution and Migration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for database schema evolution and migration strategies in the Skylink Custom Backend. Document migration planning, version control for database changes, and rollback procedures. Explain schema update processes, data migration scripts, and backward compatibility maintenance. Include database versioning strategies, environment-specific migrations, and production deployment considerations. Address schema validation, testing migration scripts, and handling complex data transformations during upgrades.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for leave request management endpoints including leave applications, approvals, and calendar operations. Document endpoints for leave request submission, approval workflows, leave balance calculations, and leave calendar views. Include request/response schemas for leave types, duration calculations, and approval status tracking. Provide examples of leave request processing and workflow management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Web MVC Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Web MVC configuration documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Document the WebConfig class for static resource serving, view resolution, and Thymeleaf template configuration. Detail the TimeZoneConfig implementation for date/time handling, timezone management, and locale configuration. Include examples of static asset management, template resolver configuration, and internationalization settings. Address web application initialization, resource mapping, and performance optimization settings. Document the integration between web configuration and the overall application architecture, including template engine setup and static content delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed employee management documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Document employee CRUD operations, department hierarchy management, supervisor-employee relationships, and reporting structures. Explain employee import/export functionality, data validation rules, and bulk operations. Include practical examples of employee workflows, department organization patterns, and integration with attendance and payroll systems. Address employee profile management, organizational chart maintenance, and data consistency requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the real-time communication system architecture. Document WebSocket configuration, bidirectional communication patterns, and live status updates. Explain push notification service implementation using Web Push Protocol, browser subscription management, and notification broadcasting mechanisms. Include WebSocket endpoint configuration, message handling patterns, and client-server communication protocols. Address scalability considerations for real-time features, connection pooling, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payroll Workflow Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the complete payroll workflow management system including status tracking, approval processes, and administrative controls. Detail workflow states from draft creation through final payment execution. Explain bulk operations, status updates, and workflow automation rules. Document role-based permissions, approval hierarchies, and audit trails. Include workflow monitoring, exception handling, and recovery procedures. Address workflow customization, escalation rules, and integration with external approval systems. Provide examples of common workflow scenarios and troubleshooting guidance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payroll API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for payroll processing endpoints including salary calculations, payslip generation, and work status management. Document endpoints for payroll computation, advance salary processing, work status updates, and payroll reporting. Include request/response formats for salary calculations, deduction computations, and payslip generation. Address payroll cycles, tax calculations, and payment processing workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database configuration documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail database connection properties, JPA/Hibernate settings, datasource configuration, and connection pooling parameters. Document development vs production database settings, schema generation options, and migration configuration. Include practical examples of database URL formatting, credential management, and connection tuning. Address database-specific configurations for PostgreSQL and H2, transaction management, and performance optimization settings. Document integration with Spring Data JPA, entity scanning, and database initialization scripts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive attendance system documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail real-time attendance tracking, ADMS device integration, multi-device status monitoring, and attendance history management. Document attendance logging mechanisms, device communication protocols, status synchronization, and historical data retrieval. Include practical examples of attendance workflows, device configuration, troubleshooting common attendance issues, and integration with leave management and payroll systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the data access layer architecture using Spring Data JPA. Document repository pattern implementation, custom query specifications, and entity relationship mappings. Explain JPA annotations usage, database schema design, and performance optimization strategies. Include criteria API specifications for complex queries, batch operations, and transaction management. Address data access patterns, caching strategies, and integration with PostgreSQL database. Document entity lifecycle management and data consistency approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Operations API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for payment request management and financial operations. Document endpoints for payment request creation, approval workflows, payment processing, and financial reporting. Include request/response schemas for payment requests, payment methods, and financial tracking. Provide examples of payment workflows, approval chains, and financial data export.

wiki_catalogs.name: External Integrations Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed external integrations configuration documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Document ADMS device communication settings, email service configuration, push notification service setup, and third-party API integrations. Detail authentication credentials, API endpoints, timeout configurations, and error handling for external services. Include practical examples of configuring device communication protocols, SMTP settings for email notifications, and Web Push Protocol (VAPID) for browser notifications. Address service availability monitoring, fallback mechanisms, and troubleshooting external integration issues. Document security considerations for external service credentials and network communication settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed leave management documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Document leave request processing workflows, calendar management, approval hierarchies, and leave quota management. Explain leave types, entitlement calculations, carry-forward rules, and integration with attendance tracking. Include practical examples of leave workflows, approval processes, calendar synchronization, and reporting capabilities. Address leave policy enforcement, audit trails, and integration with payroll systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Master Data API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for master data management endpoints including shift configurations, company data, and system settings. Document endpoints for managing lookup tables, shift schedules, company hierarchies, and system configurations. Include request/response formats for master data operations and validation rules. Address data consistency, reference data management, and configuration workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payroll System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive payroll system documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail salary calculation algorithms, payslip generation, advance salary processing, and payroll reporting. Document payroll computation rules, tax calculations, deductions, and integration with attendance and leave systems. Include practical examples of payroll workflows, calculation formulas, payslip formatting, and batch processing capabilities. Address payroll policy configuration, audit requirements, and compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for reporting and analytics endpoints including attendance reports, payroll summaries, and work order analytics. Document endpoints for generating various report types, filtering options, and data export formats. Include request/response schemas for report parameters, data aggregation, and export formats. Provide examples of report generation, filtering criteria, and data visualization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed payment operations documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Document payment request management, master data maintenance, payment method tracking, and financial reporting. Explain payment workflows, approval processes, payment scheduling, and integration with accounting systems. Include practical examples of payment request processing, dashboard functionality, export capabilities, and reconciliation procedures. Address payment security, audit trails, and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for notification management endpoints including notification creation, delivery tracking, and push notification services. Document endpoints for sending notifications, managing notification preferences, and push subscription management. Include request/response formats for notification types, delivery status, and subscription handling. Address real-time notifications, push protocol integration, and notification delivery reliability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Communication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive communication system documentation for the Skylink Custom Backend. Detail real-time notification delivery, push notification service using Web Push Protocol, email integration, and notification management. Document WebSocket implementation for live updates, notification routing, subscription management, and multi-channel communication. Include practical examples of notification workflows, push subscription handling, email configuration, and integration with other system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management API

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Skylink Custom Backend - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Skylink Custom Backend is a comprehensive attendance management and payroll processing system designed for enterprise environments. The platform provides integrated solutions for employee management, attendance tracking, leave management, payroll calculation, and real-time communication features.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Integrated Employee Management**: Centralized system for managing employee profiles, departments, and organizational hierarchy
- **Automated Payroll Processing**: Intelligent payroll calculation based on attendance, leave, and work schedules
- **Real-time Communication**: WebSocket-enabled live status monitoring and push notification capabilities
- **Multi-role Access Control**: Role-based permissions for different user types (Admin, HR, Supervisors, Employees)
- **Data Analytics**: Comprehensive reporting and dashboard visualization for management insights

### Target Audience
- **Enterprise Organizations**: Companies requiring sophisticated attendance and payroll management
- **HR Departments**: Teams managing employee records, leave policies, and compensation
- **Supervisors and Managers**: Personnel overseeing team attendance and productivity
- **Administrators**: IT staff maintaining system configurations and user access

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The system follows a layered Spring Boot architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Web Layer] --> B[Controller Layer]
    B --> C[Service Layer]
    C --> D[Repository Layer]
    D --> E[(Database)]
    
    F[Thymeleaf Templates] --> A
    G[Security Config] --> B
    H[WebSocket Config] --> A
    
    I[Push Notifications] --> C
    J[Email Service] --> C
    K[PDF Export] --> C
```

### Design Patterns
- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between Model (Entities), View (Thymeleaf templates), Controller (REST endpoints)
- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction through Spring Data JPA repositories
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic encapsulation and transaction management
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable authentication and notification systems
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time updates through WebSocket connections

### System Relationships
```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Employee {
        +String id
        +String name
        +String email
        +Department department
        +Employee reportsTo
        +Double monthlySalary
    }
    
    class Department {
        +Long id
        +String name
        +List~Employee~ employees
    }
    
    class AttendanceLog {
        +String employeeId
        +LocalDateTime timestamp
        +String deviceLocation
    }
    
    class Payslip {
        +String employeeId
        +String month
        +Double totalAmount
        +PayslipStatus status
    }
    
    Employee --> Department : belongs to
    Employee --> Employee : supervised by
    Employee --> AttendanceLog : generates
    Employee --> Payslip : creates
```

### Data Flow Diagrams
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant Controller
    participant Service
    participant Repository
    participant Database
    
    Client->>+Controller: HTTP Request
    Controller->>+Service: Business Logic Call
    Service->>+Repository: Data Query
    Repository->>+Database: SQL Query
    Database-->>-Repository: Result Set
    Repository-->>-Service: Domain Objects
    Service-->>-Controller: Processed Data
    Controller-->>-Client: Response
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Bootstrap**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/AttendanceSystemApplication.java`
- **Security Configuration**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/config/SecurityConfig.java`
- **WebSocket Configuration**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/config/WebSocketConfig.java`

### Core Modules
- **Employee Management**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/controller/EmployeeController.java`
- **Payroll Processing**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/service/PayrollService.java`
- **Attendance Tracking**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/model/AttendanceLog.java`
- **Leave Management**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/model/LeaveRequest.java`

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Configuration**: `src/main/resources/application-prod.properties`
- **Template Engine**: Thymeleaf for server-side rendering
- **Database Configuration**: PostgreSQL with H2 for development
- **File Upload Handling**: Multi-part file upload support for CSV/Excel imports

### External Dependencies
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Spring Boot 3.4.0] --> B[Core Framework]
    C[Thymeleaf 3.1.2] --> D[Template Engine]
    E[PostgreSQL Driver] --> F[Database Access]
    G[BCrypt] --> H[Password Security]
    I[OpenPDF 1.3.30] --> J[PDF Generation]
    K[Apache POI 5.2.5] --> L[Excel Processing]
    M[Web Push 5.1.1] --> N[Push Notifications]
```

### Integration Points
- **ADMS Device Integration**: Direct communication with fingerprint attendance devices
- **Email Service**: SMTP integration for notifications and reports
- **Push Notifications**: Web Push Protocol (VAPID) for browser notifications
- **File Import/Export**: CSV and Excel processing for data migration

### Component Relationship Diagrams
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Controllers] --> B[Services]
    B --> C[Repositories]
    C --> D[Entities]
    E[Security] --> A
    F[WebSocket] --> A
    G[Push Service] --> B
    H[Email Service] --> B
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Multi-role Authentication**: Admin, HR, Supervisor, and Employee roles with granular permissions
- **Real-time Attendance Monitoring**: Live floor status with WebSocket updates
- **Intelligent Payroll Calculation**: Automated salary computation based on attendance and leave
- **Comprehensive Reporting**: Monthly, weekly, and daily attendance reports
- **Device Integration**: Direct communication with ADMS fingerprint devices
- **Push Notifications**: Browser-based real-time alerts and updates

### Implementation Highlights
- **Advanced Search**: `src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/repository/EmployeeRepository.java`
- **Bulk Operations**: Employee bulk assignment and resource management
- **Live Status Updates**: WebSocket-enabled real-time employee presence tracking
- **PDF Generation**: Payroll slips and reports using OpenPDF library
- **Data Import/Export**: CSV and Excel processing for data migration

### Feature Architecture Diagrams
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Authentication
    Authentication --> Dashboard: Success
    Authentication --> [*]: Failed
    
    Dashboard --> EmployeeManagement: Admin/HR
    Dashboard --> AttendanceMonitoring: Employee
    Dashboard --> PayrollProcessing: Admin/HR
    
    EmployeeManagement --> EmployeeCRUD: Create/Update/Delete
    EmployeeCRUD --> EmployeeManagement: Complete
    
    AttendanceMonitoring --> LiveUpdates: Real-time
    LiveUpdates --> AttendanceMonitoring: Continuous
    
    PayrollProcessing --> SalaryCalculation: Generate
    SalaryCalculation --> PayrollProcessing: Complete
```

### Security Architecture
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Login] --> B{Role Check}
    B -->|Admin| C[Full Access]
    B -->|HR| D[HR Restricted Access]
    B -->|Supervisor| E[Team Management]
    B -->|Employee| F[Personal Data Only]
    
    C --> G[Dashboard]
    D --> G
    E --> G
    F --> H[Personal Profile]
    
    G --> I[Feature Access Control]
    H --> I
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
Skylink Custom Backend demonstrates a well-structured enterprise-grade attendance management system built on Spring Boot. The architecture effectively separates concerns through MVC pattern implementation, provides robust security through role-based access control, and offers real-time capabilities through WebSocket integration.

### Key Characteristics
- **Scalable Architecture**: Modular design allows for easy feature addition and maintenance
- **Enterprise Security**: Comprehensive role-based permissions and secure authentication
- **Real-time Capabilities**: WebSocket integration enables live status monitoring
- **Data Integrity**: Strong typing with Lombok annotations and validation
- **Extensible Design**: Plugin-friendly architecture for additional integrations

### Architectural Strengths
- Clear separation of layers with well-defined responsibilities
- Comprehensive security implementation with custom authentication handlers
- Real-time communication capabilities through WebSocket and push notifications
- Flexible reporting and export capabilities
- Multi-environment support (development, production)

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Enhanced Testing Coverage**: Expand unit and integration test suites
- **API Documentation**: Implement OpenAPI/Swagger documentation
- **Performance Optimization**: Add caching mechanisms for frequently accessed data
- **Monitoring and Logging**: Implement comprehensive logging and metrics collection
- **Error Handling**: Standardize error responses across all controllers

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Comprehensive Testing**: Add extensive unit tests for business logic and integration tests for API endpoints
2. **Add API Documentation**: Generate OpenAPI documentation for better developer experience
3. **Performance Enhancement**: Implement Redis caching for frequently accessed employee and department data
4. **Monitoring Setup**: Add Spring Boot Actuator and Prometheus metrics for system monitoring
5. **Security Hardening**: Implement rate limiting and enhanced audit logging

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Microservices Migration**: Consider breaking down monolithic structure for better scalability
- **Cloud Native Deployment**: Containerization with Docker and Kubernetes orchestration
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement machine learning for attendance pattern prediction
- **Mobile Application**: Develop companion mobile app for field supervisors
- **Third-party Integrations**: Add integration APIs for popular HRIS platforms

**Sources:**
- [AttendanceSystemApplication.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/AttendanceSystemApplication.java)
- [build.gradle](build.gradle)
- [application-prod.properties](src/main/resources/application-prod.properties)
- [SecurityConfig.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/config/SecurityConfig.java)
- [Employee.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/model/Employee.java)
- [EmployeeController.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/controller/EmployeeController.java)
- [PayrollService.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/service/PayrollService.java)
- [dashboard.html](src/main/resources/templates/dashboard.html)
- [LoginController.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/controller/LoginController.java)
- [EmployeeRepository.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/repository/EmployeeRepository.java)
- [WebSocketConfig.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/config/WebSocketConfig.java)
- [PushNotificationService.java](src/main/java/root/cyb/mh/attendancesystem/service/PushNotificationService.java)

</blog>
